Diesel variants included 2.5L TDI 5-cylinder engines (engine codes AAT, AHN) with outputs from around 110 to 150 hp. V8 engines and high-performance variants were not present in 1997. engineSummary: Main engines include the 2.5L inline-4 gasoline (A25A-FKS) with D-4S direct and port injection, a 2.0L turbocharged inline-4 (M20A-FKS) in some markets, a 2.5L hybrid inline-4 (M20A-FXS) using Toyota Hybrid System II, and in select markets a 2.5L 4-cylinder (2AR-FE) for cost-sensitive variants.
power: Approximately 203 hp (2.5L A25A-FKS), 206 hp (2.0L turbo M20A-FKS), and combined output for hybrid of about 208 hp (M20A-FXS)
torque: Approx. 184 lb-ft (2.5L A25A-FKS), 258 lb-ft (2.0L turbo M20A-FKS), hybrid torque varies
fuelSystem: D-4S direct and port fuel injection (gasoline models)
recommendedOil: API SN or higher, SAE 0W-16 or 0W-20 (market dependent, follow owner's manual)
oilCapacity: Approx. 4.6 liters (service fill with filter change)
coolantCapacity: Approx. 6.7 liters (engine and radiator system combined)
manualTransmission: Not generally available for 2023 Camry globally; most markets have automatic
automaticTransmission: 8-speed Direct-Shift 8AT or Continuously Variable Transmission (CVT) for hybrid models
transmissionFluid: Toyota WS (World Standard) or SEC synthetic ATF for automatic transmissions
differentialFluid: Not typically serviceable on FWD trims; AWD versions use specific gear oil per manual
brakeFluid: DOT 3 or DOT 4 depending on market
sparkPlugs: Iridium tipped spark plugs, gap ~0.044 inches (1.1 mm)
serviceInterval: Typically 10,000 miles (16,000 km) or 12 months, varies by market and usage
commonProblems: Minor electronic glitches in infotainment
Brake wear on higher mileage hybrids
firstChecks: Check hybrid battery health if hybrid model
Inspect ATF quality in automatic transmissions regularly
notes: Hybrid and engine options vary significantly by market.
Technical specifications may differ slightly depending on emissions and regulatory compliance.
sourceNotes: Toyota Global Press Kits and Technical Manuals 2023
Automotive technical reviews and EPA Certifications 2023

market: Global (variations exist by region including North America, Asia, Australia)
engineSummary: 2024 Toyota Camry mid-cycle refresh continues with mostly 4-cylinder gasoline engines: A25A-FKS 2.5L naturally aspirated I4, M20A-FKS 2.0L naturally aspirated I4 (Asia), and A25A-FXS 2.5L hybrid engine variant.
power: 203-206 hp for 2.5L NA, 168 hp for 2.0L, combined 208 hp for hybrid
torque: 184-186 lb-ft typical for 2.5L, 199 Nm for 2.0L, combined approx. 163 lb-ft for hybrid
fuelSystem: Multi-point EFI with D-4S system for 2.5L A25A engines
recommendedOil: API SN or higher, SAE 0W-20 synthetic typically recommended
oilCapacity: Approximately 4.6 liters service-fill including filter (varies slightly by engine, confirm per market service manual)
coolantCapacity: Around 7.2 liters depending on model and radiator option
manualTransmission: Not available on most markets; some Asia trims offer 6-speed manual on 2.0L
automaticTransmission: 8-speed automatic for 2.5L gas engines, CVT for hybrid and some 2.0L models
transmissionFluid: Toyota Genuine ATF WS for automatic transmissions
differentialFluid: Not applicable (FWD typical); AWD variants use designated synthetic gear oil
brakeFluid: DOT 3 or DOT 4 depending on market
sparkPlugs: Iridium tipped recommended, gap approx 0.044 inch (1.1 mm)
serviceInterval: 10,000 miles or 12 months typical; consult local schedule
commonProblems: Minor early oil consumption noted on some 2.5L engines in high-mileage applications
CVT software issues occasionally reported in hybrid versions
firstChecks: Inspect oil consumption on 2.5L engines
Check transmission fluid level and condition on automatics
notes: Hybrid model uses A25A-FXS engine with integrated electric motor and CVT.
Technical profiles can vary by market; North America mostly A25A-FKS-based gasoline and hybrid, Asia sees additional 2.0L M20A-FKS variant.
sourceNotes: Official Toyota global specifications and service manuals 2023-2024
Reviewed technical data from international automotive press and Toyota regional websites

market: Global (US, Japan, Australia, Europe, Southeast Asia variations)
engineSummary: Inline-4 2.5L naturally aspirated petrol (A25A-FKS), hybrid petrol engine (A25A-FXS), inline-4 2.0L naturally aspirated petrol (M20A-FKS in some markets)
displacement: 2487 cc (2.5L) or 1987 cc (2.0L depending on market)
power: 203-206 hp (2.5L NA petrol), 176 hp combined (hybrid 2.5L), approx. 167 hp (2.0L NA petrol)
torque: 250 Nm (2.5L NA petrol), 221 Nm (hybrid 2.5L), 199 Nm (2.0L petrol variant)
fuelSystem: Electronic fuel injection (D-4S system on 2.5L inline-4, includes direct and port injection)
timingDrive: Chain-driven dual VVT-i and VVT-iE (variable valve timing)
recommendedOil: SAE 0W-16 or 5W-30 API SN or higher; confirmed synthetic oil recommended
oilCapacity: Approximately 4.5 liters service fill with filter change (varies slightly by engine variant and market)
coolantCapacity: Approx. 6.5 liters (including radiator and engine block)
manualTransmission: Not offered in most markets on 2025 Camry; mainly automatic or CVT (for hybrid)
transmissionFluid: Toyota Genuine ATF WS for 8-speed automatic; Hybrid CVT fluid specific to model
differentialFluid: Not applicable for FWD; no AWD variant with mechanical differential available globally
brakeFluid: DOT 3 or DOT 4 as per regional specifications
sparkPlugs: Iridium tip type recommended, change interval approx. 100,000 km
serviceInterval: Oil change every 10,000 km or 12 months; other maintenance per owner's manual
tireSizes: 225/55R17 (base), 235/45R18 (upper trims) - varies by market and trim
commonProblems: No major widespread issues reported at this release stage; some markets reported early minor electronics quirks
firstChecks: Verify regional engine code variant due to multiple engine offerings globally
Check hybrid system health if applicable
notes: Global model varies slightly by market with respect to available engines and trims.
Hybrid version (A25A-FXS) includes an electric motor integrated with a planetary gearset e-CVT.
sourceNotes: Toyota official global press releases 2024-2025 for Camry XV70 refresh
Multiple authoritative automotive technical reviews and global market specifications databases (e.g., Japan and US EPA certification filings)

market: Japan, North America, Europe, Australia
engineSummary: Inline-4 engines: 2.0L 3S-GE (performance model), 2.0L 3S-FE (standard), and 2.2L 5S-FE in some markets
power: 140-180 hp depending on engine and market
torque: 135-140 lb-ft depending on engine and market
recommendedOil: API SJ or newer, 5W-30 or 10W-30 depending on climate
oilCapacity: Approximately 4.3 liters (service-fill including filter)
manualTransmission: 5-speed manual (most trims), 6-speed manual for some JDM GT-Four versions
transmissionFluid: Toyota Genuine ATF or equivalent for automatics
differentialFluid: API GL-5 hypoid gear oil for rear differential (if equipped)
sparkPlugs: Standard gap 0.044 in (1.1 mm), NGK or Denso recommended
serviceInterval: Every 5,000 to 7,500 miles or 6 months depending on market conditions
commonProblems: Timing belt replacement critical to avoid engine damage
Suspension bushings wear over time
firstChecks: Inspect timing belt condition
Check for coolant leaks
Check suspension and steering components
notes: The 1996 Toyota Celica T200 generation covers multiple trims and engine options varying by market.
GT-Four turbocharged AWD variant existed but was rare and market-limited; this profile focuses on the main global naturally aspirated versions.
sourceNotes: Toyota Celica T200 technical specifications from official Toyota brochures and service manuals 1996
Verified market availability and engine codes cross-referenced with automotive databases and enthusiast archives

market: Global (Japan, North America, Europe, Australia)
engineSummary: 1.8L 3S-GE DOHC 16-valve inline-4 (performance GT/GT-S trims in many markets), 2.0L 5S-FE SOHC inline-4 (Japan ST202 sport versions), and 2.0L 3S-FE SOHC inline-4 (common NA and Europe non-sport variants)
power: 140-180 hp depending on engine and market
recommendedOil: API SL/CF SAE 5W-30 synthetic recommended
oilCapacity: 4.5 liters (including filter) - service-fill with oil filter change
transmissionFluid: Toyota ATF Type T-IV for automatic, manual requires API GL-4 gear oil
differentialFluid: API GL-5 gear oil, approx. 1.2 liters
sparkPlugs: NGK BKR6E or equivalent, gap 0.9 mm
serviceInterval: Every 5,000 to 10,000 km depending on market and use
commonProblems: Timing belt maintenance critical
Coil-pack ignition issues
Rust on rear quarter panels in humid climates
firstChecks: Timing belt condition and service history
Check for engine oil leaks around valve cover
Inspect suspension bushings for wear
notes: Engine and exact specifications vary noticeably by market: Japanese domestic market featured 5S-FE 2.0L inline-4 versions with some sport GT trims using 3S-GE, North America mostly 3S-GE and 3S-FE engines.
Oil capacity stated includes filter change for a full service fill.
sourceNotes: Toyota Celica T200 Factory Service Manual excerpts
Global market vehicle brochures and technical data compilations

market: Global (Japan, Europe, North America, Oceania)
engineSummary: Three main petrol engines: 2.0L DOHC I4 3S-GE high-performance, 2.0L SOHC I4 3S-FE inline-4 economy, and 2.2L SOHC I4 5S-FE for some regions.
recommendedOil: API grade 5W-30 or 10W-30, depending on climate
oilCapacity: Approximately 4.2 liters with filter (service fill including filter recommended)
transmissionFluid: Toyota Genuine ATF Type T-IV (automatic), API GL-4 rated gear oil for manual
differentialFluid: API GL-5 rated gear oil, approx. 0.9 liters
sparkPlugs: NGK Platinum recommended
serviceInterval: Every 5,000 to 10,000 miles depending on market and usage
commonProblems: Timing belt wear (replace at recommended intervals)
Front suspension bush wear
Rust in some markets
firstChecks: Timing belt condition
Cooling system hoses
Brake condition and fluid levels
relatedCodes: ST204 chassis variations depending on market
notes: Market variations include engine availability and minor specification differences, e.g., 3S-GE more common in Japan and Europe while North America also had 5S-FE options.
Oil capacity values include service fill and filter replacement for accuracy.
sourceNotes: Toyota official parts catalogs and service manuals for 1998 Celica models
Verified enthusiast and technical community databases such as CelicaTech and global Toyota archives

market: Global (mainly Japan, North America, Europe, Asia)
engineSummary: Main engines for 1999 Celica included the 1.8L 1ZZ-FE and 2.0L 3S-GE inline-4 gasoline engines in various markets; the sportier GT-S variant featured the higher-output 2.0L DOHC 2ZZ-GE in select markets like Japan and Europe.
power: 140-180 hp depending on engine and market
torque: 127-152 Nm depending on engine and market
timingDrive: Timing chain on 1ZZ-FE and 2ZZ-GE, timing belt on 3S-GE
recommendedOil: SAE 5W-30 API SL or higher grade
oilCapacity: Approx. 3.7 L including oil filter (service-fill with filter change)
transmissionFluid: Toyota Genuine ATF WS for automatic; API GL-4 gear oil for manual
differentialFluid: 75W-90 gear oil for limited-slip differential models
sparkPlugs: Iridium or platinum tipped recommended, gap ~0.8-0.9 mm
serviceInterval: Oil change every 5,000-7,500 miles (8,000-12,000 km) depending on conditions
commonProblems: Head gasket leaks in higher mileage 3S-GE engines
Timing belt wear on 3S-GE engine needs regular replacement
Suspension bushing wear
firstChecks: Check timing belt condition if 3S-GE engine
Inspect valve cover gasket for leaks
Verify automatic transmission fluid level and condition
notes: Worldwide markets had some variations in equipment and engine fitment; US models typically came with 1ZZ-FE or 2ZZ-GE engines depending on trim.
The 2ZZ-GE engine features VVTL-i (variable valve timing and lift) technology with a timing chain, unlike the 3S-GE which uses a timing belt.
sourceNotes: Toyota Celica 7th generation official technical specifications booklet 1999
World automotive engine database and enthusiast technical forums cross-referenced

market: Global (USA, Japan, Europe, Australia)
engineSummary: The 2000 Toyota Celica was offered primarily with naturally aspirated inline-4 engines, with variations by market: 1.8L 1ZZ-FE in the base and GT trims (common in USA, Europe, Australia), the 2.0L 3S-GE in some Japanese and European versions, and the higher-output 1.8L 2ZZ-GE engine in the GT-S / SS-II trims mostly for USA and Japan.
recommendedOil: API SL or better, SAE 5W-30 recommended
oilCapacity: Approx. 4.1 L (service fill with filter change for 1ZZ-FE and 2ZZ-GE); refer to specific engine manual
transmissionFluid: Toyota ATF Type T-IV for automatic; API GL-4 for manual
differentialFluid: API GL-5 SAE 75W-90 synthetic recommended
sparkPlugs: Iridium or platinum plugs recommended, gap approx. 0.044 in (1.1 mm)
serviceInterval: 10,000 miles / 12 months typical
commonProblems: Timing belt wear on 3S-GE variant
Oil consumption in high mileage 2ZZ-GE engines
Suspension wear in older models
firstChecks: Timing belt condition (3S-GE)
Valve cover gasket leaks
Ignition coil resistance and spark plug condition
notes: Different markets had different engine offerings; the 1ZZ-FE is most common globally, with the 2ZZ-GE reserved for sportier trims.
The 3S-GE engine uses a timing belt, unlike the timing chain of the other engines.
sourceNotes: Toyota Celica 2000 owner's manual and official Toyota USA parts catalog
Encyclopedia of Toyota Celica by automotive historian and Toyota service data

market: Global (main markets include Japan, North America, Europe, Australia)
engineSummary: The 2001 Toyota Celica was offered primarily with four 4-cylinder engines globally: the 2.0L 3S-GE inline-4 (performance-oriented, common in Japan and some European markets), the 1.8L 1ZZ-FE inline-4 (common in North America and Europe), the high-revving 1.8L 2ZZ-GE inline-4 (used in the GT-S trim in North America and Japan), and the 2.0L 3S-FE inline-4 (less common, found in some export markets).
power: 140-190 hp depending on engine and market
torque: 132-143 Nm depending on engine and market
recommendedOil: SAE 5W-30 or SAE 5W-40, API SL or higher
oilCapacity: Approx. 4.2L service fill including filter
manualTransmission: 5-speed manual (6-speed manual available with 2ZZ-GE in some markets)
transmissionFluid: Toyota Dexron II or equivalent automatic transmission fluid for AT; API GL-4 75W-90 for manuals
differentialFluid: API GL-5 75W-90 gear oil
sparkPlugs: Standard NGK or Denso recommended for each engine type
serviceInterval: Oil and filter every 5,000-7,500 miles (market dependent); timing belt replacement every 60,000 miles
commonProblems: Timing belt wear (critical to replace on schedule)
Valve seat wear in 3S-GE engines
Suspension bushings wear causing noise
firstChecks: Verify timing belt condition and replacement history
Inspect for head gasket leaks in 3S-GE variants
Check for rust in European models
notes: The Celica T230 underwent a facelift in 2000; 2001 models may reflect this update depending on market.
Engine availability varies significantly by market; North America favored 1ZZ-FE and 2ZZ-GE, Japan had multiple engine options including 3S-GE.
sourceNotes: Toyota USA Owner’s Manual and Technical Specifications 2001 Celica
Global automotive databases and enthusiast technical forums documenting T230 Celica variants

market: Global (primarily Japan, North America, Europe)
engineSummary: Available with 1.8L 1ZZ-FE I4 (base), 1.8L high-rev 2ZZ-GE I4 (GT-S), and 2.0L 3S-GE I4 (some Asia markets).
power: Approx. 130-180 hp depending on engine and market
recommendedOil: SAE 5W-30 or 5W-40 meeting API SL or higher
oilCapacity: Approximately 4.3L service-fill with filter change (varies slightly by engine)
transmissionFluid: Toyota WS automatic transmission fluid for automatics; standard MT fluid for manuals

commonProblems: Timing belt wear on 3S-GE requires replacement at recommended intervals
2ZZ-GE cam followers wear on high mileage cars
Coolant leaks around water pump
firstChecks: Timing belt condition for 3S-GE engines
Cam follower noise on 2ZZ-GE
Suspension bushings and ball joints wear
notes: Market variations exist: North America primarily had 1ZZ-FE and 2ZZ-GE engines, Asia also offered 3S-GE in some trims.
Some later 2002 models introduced facelift updates, including revised body panels and interior features.

Automotive review and technical guides: Haynes Celica (2000-2005 edition)

market: Global (mainly Japan, North America, Europe, Australia)
engineSummary: Inline-4 engines including high-performance 2ZZ-GE (1.8L), standard 1ZZ-FE (1.8L), and turbocharged 3S-GTE (2.0L) in select markets.
power: 2ZZ-GE: approx. 180-190 PS / 177-187 hp; 1ZZ-FE: approx. 140 hp; 3S-GTE: approx. 210 hp (Japan/Europe)
recommendedOil: SAE 5W-30 or 5W-40 synthetic recommended for 2ZZ-GE; consult owner's manual per market
oilCapacity: Approx. 3.7 to 4.3 liters with filter change (varies by engine code), service-fill including oil filter replacement
coolantCapacity: Approx. 6.5 liters with complete system fill
manualTransmission: 6-speed Getrag F23 (2ZZ-GE models) / 5-speed manual in other versions
transmissionFluid: ATF Type T-IV (automatic); manual gear oil recommended per manual
differentialFluid: GL-5 gear oil, typically SAE 75W-90
sparkPlugs: Iridium recommended (NGK PFR6Q or equivalent), gap 0.9mm
serviceInterval: Every 7,500-10,000 miles (12,000-16,000 km) typical; timing chain generally maintenance-free

3S-GTE head gasket issues in high mileage units
Rear quarter window seal leaks in some markets

Check for any oil leaks around head gasket area (3S-GTE)
Verify condition of suspension bushings and rear window seals
notes: The 2003 Celica was the final model year of the seventh generation (T230).
Availability of engine trims varies by market: e.g., 3S-GTE mainly Japan and Europe; North America had 1ZZ-FE and 2ZZ-GE.

Automotive industry reviews and verified databases (Global Car Data, JATO Dynamics).

market: Global except North America (last US model year was 2000)
engineSummary: Fourth generation Celica continued with two main engines globally: a 1.8L inline-4 (1ZZ-FE) producing around 140 PS, and a sportier 1.8L high-revving inline-4 (2ZZ-GE) used in higher trims. North America did not receive the Celica after 2000.
recommendedOil: API SL 5W-30 recommended
oilCapacity: Approx. 4.2 liters (including oil filter); service-fill capacity with oil filter change
automaticTransmission: 4-speed automatic available with 1ZZ-FE engine in some markets
transmissionFluid: Toyota Type T-IV automatic transmission fluid or equivalent
differentialFluid: Hypoid gear oil SAE 75W-90 recommended
sparkPlugs: Iridium tipped spark plugs (NGK recommended); gap approx. 0.9 mm
serviceInterval: Every 10,000 km or 12 months, whichever comes first
commonProblems: Clutch wear on manual transmission models; throttle body build-up affecting idle; rear suspension bushings wear
firstChecks: Inspect timing chain tensioner for noise; check condition of ignition coils; verify absence of oil leaks around valve cover gasket
notes: 2004 was the final model year in most markets except Europe and Japan where limited sales continued to 2005 model year.
Market variations included small differences in emissions equipment and available transmissions.
sourceNotes: Toyota global product brochures and technical manuals 2003-2005
Independent automotive service guides and enthusiast forums for T230 Toyota Celica

market: Global / primarily Japan, Europe, Australia, and North America
engineSummary: Main engines in 2005 Celica were the 1.8L 2ZZ-GE (high-performance, VVT-i) and the 1.8L 1ZZ-FE (standard, VVT-i). Availability varied by market: 2ZZ-GE more common in North America, Japan, and Europe sports trims; 1ZZ-FE in base models and some markets.
power: 190 PS (2ZZ-GE) / 140 PS (1ZZ-FE) depending on engine and market
recommendedOil: API SN or higher, SAE 5W-30
oilCapacity: 4.4 liters (service-fill including oil filter change for 2ZZ-GE)
automaticTransmission: 4-speed automatic with sequential mode (varied by market and engine)
transmissionFluid: Toyota WS (World Standard) automatic transmission fluid for AT
differentialFluid: API GL-5 gear oil (75W-90 commonly used)
sparkPlugs: Iridium type recommended for 2ZZ-GE, standard plugs for 1ZZ-FE
serviceInterval: Oil and filter every 5,000-7,500 miles / 8,000-12,000 km, timing chain maintenance not required under normal usage
commonProblems: Carbon build-up in 2ZZ-GE under certain urban/mixed driving conditions
Clutch wear in manual transmissions
Ignition coil failure on 2ZZ-GE after high mileage
firstChecks: Confirm engine code from stamped block or VIN to verify 2ZZ-GE or 1ZZ-FE
Check timing chain noise or rattle
Inspect for oil leaks around valve cover gasket and oil pan
notes: 2005 was the final production year for the Celica globally, discontinued after the T230 generation.
Specifications vary slightly by market due to emissions and trim differences.
No official 'technical-profile' trim; this profile reflects the main 2005 technical specifications.
sourceNotes: Toyota official service manuals and parts catalogues for 2005 Celica T230.

market: North America, Japan, select global markets

differentialFluid: Not applicable for FWD; check AWD variants individually

Check for oil leaks around valve cover gasket
